Document:
# Mothperson
[bold]Mothpeople[/bold] are an [bold][color=#4df]Oxygen-breathing[/color][/bold], [bold][color=#6f0]Insect-blooded[/color][/bold] species; with distinct antennae, large wings, and fine scales.
Their large wings grant them [bold]increased acceleration in low gravity[/bold], but don't allow free movement in space.
Moths take [bold]almost double the damage while [color=#f44]on fire[/color][/bold] compared to other species!
Mothpeople have the baseline body temperature of [bold][color=yellow]37°C[/color][/bold] (~[color=yellow]310°K[/color]). They experience the effects of [bold]hypothermia at [color=#4df]-43°C[/color][/bold] and [bold]hyperthermia at [color=#f44]47°C[/color][/bold]. (~[color=#4df]260°K[/color] & [color=#f44]320°K[/color])
Mothpeople have a [bold][color=#ff0]highly specialized metabolism[/color][/bold], they [bold]cannot eat conventional meals[/bold] - subsisting off of [bold][color=#ff0]fiber[/color][/bold], that they can get from eating [bold]clothing and paper[/bold].
Their bodies can process [bold]2 reagents[/bold] at a time.
They slowly [bold]passively regenerate[/bold] all types of [bold][color=yellow]Brute[/color]damage [/bold], and individually [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color]damage [/bold].
Mothperson names typically follow a "[bold]Firstname Lastname[/bold]" pattern, commonly featuring themes related to space, and mentioning their wings, coats, or other features.
